---
title: "Parameter Sweeps"
subtitle: "Evaluate one structure with several parameter sets"
jupyter: python3
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: show
    code-tools: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  error: false
---

This tutorial constructs one diblock and varies only `Rg_blockB`. It assumes
the Python environment from the [diblock tutorial](diblock-copolymer.qmd).

## Python

```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import pyseb

world = pyseb.World("SweepTutorial")
graph = world.Add("GaussianPolymer", "blockA")
world.Link("GaussianPolymer", "blockB.end1", "blockA.end2")
world.Add(graph, "diblock")

q = np.geomspace(1e-3, 0.5, 180)
base = {
    "beta_blockA": 1.0,
    "beta_blockB": 0.8,
    "Rg_blockA": 5.0,
}

python_sweeps = {}
for rg_b in (4.0, 8.0, 16.0):
    parameters = {**base, "Rg_blockB": rg_b}
    python_sweeps[rg_b] = np.asarray(
        world.EvaluateFormFactor("diblock", parameters, q.tolist())
    )
    print(f"Rg_blockB={rg_b:g}: F({q[-1]:g})={python_sweeps[rg_b][-1]:.8f}")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-python-parameter-sweep
#| fig-cap: "Diblock form factor for three radii of gyration of block B."

for rg_b, values in python_sweeps.items():
    plt.loglog(q, values, label=fr"$R_{{g,B}}={rg_b:g}$")

plt.xlabel(r"$q$")
plt.ylabel(r"$F(q)$")
plt.legend()
plt.show()
```

## C++

The C++ program writes CSV, making its output suitable for plotting or further
analysis.

```cpp
{{< include cpp/parameter-sweep.cpp >}}
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-cpp-parameter-sweep-page
#| fig-cap: "C++ sweep output captured and plotted during this documentation build."
#| echo: false

import csv
import io

from _helpers import run_tutorial

output = run_tutorial("parameter-sweep")
rows = list(csv.DictReader(io.StringIO(output)))

for rg_b in sorted({float(row["Rg_blockB"]) for row in rows}):
    selected = [row for row in rows if float(row["Rg_blockB"]) == rg_b]
    plt.loglog(
        [float(row["q"]) for row in selected],
        [float(row["F(q)"]) for row in selected],
        marker="o",
        label=fr"$R_{{g,B}}={rg_b:g}$",
    )

plt.xlabel(r"$q$")
plt.ylabel(r"$F(q)$")
plt.legend()
plt.show()
```

## Next steps

- [Combine a PDB cloud with a polymer](pdb-polymer.qmd)
- [Scattering quantities](https://github.com/Tobionecenobi/pySEB/wiki/Scattering-Quantities)
- [Python user guide](https://github.com/Tobionecenobi/pySEB/wiki/Python-User-Guide)
